name: Alexi Giannoulias
bio: Alexi Giannoulias has served as Illinois Secretary of State since January 2023, elected in November 2022. He previously served as Illinois State Treasurer from 2007 to 2011. In 2010, he ran for the U.S. Senate seat vacated by Barack Obama, losing to Republican Mark Kirk by 2 percentage points. His family owned Broadway Bank in Chicago, which failed in April 2010 during the financial crisis — an issue that dogged his 2010 Senate campaign. As Secretary of State, he has focused on driver's license access, voting rights, and digitizing state services.
prior_office: Illinois Secretary of State (2023–present); Illinois State Treasurer (2007–2011)

jacket.note: Running uncontested in Democratic primary. Finance data for Secretary of State race pending ILSBE lookup.

red_flags.title: Broadway Bank failure — family bank collapsed during 2010 financial crisis
red_flags.description: Broadway Bank, owned by the Giannoulias family, was seized by federal regulators and failed in April 2010. The bank had made loans to convicted criminals including Tony Rezko and Michael 'Jaws' Giorango, and had expanded its risky loan portfolio significantly under Alexi's tenure as senior loan officer (2002-2006) before he became State Treasurer. The failure became a major issue in his 2010 U.S. Senate race.

news_hits.title: 2026 Illinois Primary Live Results
news_hits.summary: 270toWin election day results tracker confirms Alexi Giannoulias is unopposed in the Democratic primary for his second term as Illinois Secretary of State.

news_hits.title: Illinois Secretary Of State Race: Primary Election 2026
news_hits.summary: Two Republican candidates competing in the March 17 primary for the chance to face Democrat incumbent Alexi Giannoulias in the November general election.

news_hits.title: Your guide to the March 2026 primary in DuPage County
news_hits.summary: Alexi Giannoulias, incumbent Democrat, has filed to run for reelection as Illinois Secretary of State. Walter Adamczyk and Diane M. Harris filed as Republican challengers.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias runs unopposed in Democratic Secretary of State primary
news_hits.summary: Incumbent Democratic Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias is running unopposed in his primary for reelection, automatically advancing to the November general election. For the GOP nomination for secretary of state, Joliet resident Diane M. Harris is competing against Chicagoan Walter Adamczyk. The winner will face Giannoulias in November; Giannoulias is a heavy favorite in the blue state.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton, Daniel Biss, Darren Bailey among winners in 2026 Illinois primary elections
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times election night recap confirms Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic Secretary of State primary, advancing automatically to the November general election. Giannoulias was first elected in 2022 and is seeking his second term.

news_hits.title: Illinois Primary Election Takeaways: Stratton Wins and AIPAC's Power Is Tested
news_hits.summary: Post-primary coverage of statewide races confirms Giannoulias advances unopposed as Democratic Secretary of State nominee for November 2026.

news_hits.title: Who won the 2026 Illinois primary election? Latest results, and which races remain too close to call
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago primary results roundup confirms incumbent Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic primary. He will face Republican Diane Harris (who secured the GOP nomination) in the November general election. With no primary challenge, Giannoulias is positioned as a strong favorite in the heavily Democratic state.

news_hits.title: Alexi Giannoulias runs unopposed for second term as Illinois Secretary of State
news_hits.summary: Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic primary, advancing automatically to the November general election where he will seek his second four-year term. He will face Republican nominee Diane Harris of Joliet, who defeated Walter Adamczyk in the GOP primary.

news_hits.title: Two low-funded Republicans battle to run against Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias in the fall
news_hits.summary: Tribune preview of the Republican primary to oppose Giannoulias in November. Notes two underfunded Republicans contested the GOP primary while Giannoulias ran unopposed on the Democratic side. In the 2022 general election Giannoulias won with 54% over Republican Dan Brady. He is heavily favored for reelection in the November general election.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias Cruises: Unopposed in Democratic Primary, GOP Brawls for Illinois Secretary of State
news_hits.summary: Alexi Giannoulias advances unopposed in the Democratic primary. On the Republican side, Diane Harris and Walter Adamczyk contested the GOP nomination. Giannoulias heads to the general as a heavy favorite.

news_hits.title: Illinois Secretary of State Primary Election 2026 Live Results
news_hits.summary: NBC News confirms Giannoulias is unopposed in the Democratic primary as he seeks a second term as Illinois Secretary of State.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias cash surge fuels City Hall buzz
news_hits.summary: Politico Illinois Playbook reports Alexi Giannoulias is accumulating substantial campaign cash ahead of his unopposed Secretary of State primary, with growing speculation he may be positioning for a 2027 Chicago mayoral run. The cash buildup is unusual for an uncontested incumbent, fueling political chatter about his broader ambitions.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias banks $9M amid mayoral buzz
news_hits.summary: Politico Illinois Playbook reports a scoop: Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias has logged a stunning $9 million in campaign contributions — an extraordinary amount for an uncontested statewide incumbent — fueling growing talk that he is laying the groundwork to run for mayor of Chicago in 2027. Giannoulias faces no Democratic primary opponent and is a heavy November general election favorite against Republican Diane Harris.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias Joins Illinois Literacy Foundation, YMCA to Celebrate National Read Across America Day
news_hits.summary: Secretary of State Giannoulias joined the Illinois Literacy Foundation and YMCA of Metropolitan Chicago for a Day of Service on March 2, 2026 — National Read Across America Day — donating books and participating in literacy programming. Official press release from the SOS office.

news_hits.title: Alexi Giannoulias wins Democratic primary for Illinois Secretary of State unopposed
news_hits.summary: Incumbent Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the March 17, 2026 Democratic primary, securing renomination. He will face Republican nominee Diane Harris in the November general election. The race is considered solidly Democratic.

news_hits.title: Illinois House Passes Giannoulias' Auto Insurance Reform Bill
news_hits.summary: The Illinois House passed Senate Bill 1486, a consumer protection measure led by Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias to reform the state's auto insurance industry. The bill requires insurers to provide at least 60 days' notice before raising renewal premiums by more than 10%, expands prohibitions on discriminatory rate-setting, and gives the Illinois Department of Insurance greater authority to review rate filings. The legislation stems from Giannoulias' statewide 'Driving Change' campaign highlighting how insurers use socioeconomic factors like credit scores and ZIP codes to set rates that disproportionately impact seniors and working families.

news_hits.title: Illinois Democrats ran on anti-Trump messaging ahead of general election — Giannoulias among winners gearing up for November
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times post-primary analysis by Lynn Sweet notes Giannoulias is among the Democratic nominees now turning their anti-Trump primary messaging toward the November general election. The piece cautions that without actual congressional power, Democrats must translate campaign slogans into concrete opposition strategies. Giannoulias, as Secretary of State, is in a lower-stakes race with no prominent Republican challenger, making his path to re-election straightforward.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton, La Shawn Ford and other Illinois primary winners who beat deeper pockets
news_hits.summary: Rich Miller's post-primary column notes that Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic primary for Secretary of State, positioning himself as a general election incumbent facing Republican Diane Harris in November. Giannoulias avoided primary spending and enters the general election with a financial advantage and high name recognition from his 2010 U.S. Senate run.

news_hits.title: Illinois General Assembly passes Giannoulias' auto insurance reform bill
news_hits.summary: The Illinois House passed Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias' auto insurance reform legislation, which previously cleared the Senate. The bill updates minimum liability coverage requirements for the first time in decades, requiring drivers to carry higher minimums to better protect accident victims. Giannoulias framed the bill as a consumer protection measure, noting Illinois' current $20,000/$40,000 minimums are woefully inadequate given modern medical costs. The legislation is expected to be signed by Gov. Pritzker.

news_hits.title: Pritzker quips on weight loss, Rahm and 2028 buzz in Washington speech
news_hits.summary: Gov. Pritzker's Gridiron Club speech and post-primary political positioning is relevant to Giannoulias as IL Secretary of State — Pritzker is the dominant force in Illinois Democratic politics and his priorities (transit funding, immigrant protection, 2028 presidential aspirations) directly shape the legislative and political environment Giannoulias operates in as a statewide officer.

news_hits.title: State lawmakers advance bill to keep ICE detention centers out of communities
news_hits.summary: An Illinois House committee advanced legislation barring new ICE detention centers from residential neighborhoods — part of a broader wave of Illinois state immigration bills in the post-Midway Blitz political environment. As Secretary of State, Giannoulias oversees driver's license policy, voter registration, and other state functions that intersect with immigrant communities.

news_hits.title: Primary voter turnout numbers — high for Democrats, low for Republicans — may guide future trends
news_hits.summary: Rich Miller's post-primary analysis confirms a strong Democratic environment in Illinois ahead of November. Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic Secretary of State primary and faces only token GOP opposition in November. The broad Democratic turnout surge across suburban collar counties reinforces his path to a second term as Secretary of State.

news_hits.title: Trump to sign order paying TSA agents as DHS funding standoff drags on
news_hits.summary: The DHS funding standoff has placed ICE agents at Illinois airports including O'Hare. Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias has been an advocate for immigrant access to state IDs and driver's licenses — immigration enforcement proximity at airports highlights the ongoing policy tension between federal enforcement and state-level services his office administers.

news_hits.title: Florida flip sends chills through Illinois GOP
news_hits.summary: Illinois Republicans growing alarmed as Democrats outperform two-to-one in Kane and DuPage county primary turnout, mirroring a Florida seat flip from a GOP-held district near Mar-a-Lago. GOP operatives warn the trend could spell serious trouble in November.

news_hits.title: Pritzker brings heat — and 2028 buzz
news_hits.summary: Gov. JB Pritzker, fresh off backing two winning primary candidates including Juliana Stratton, attended the Gridiron Club dinner in D.C. and raised his national profile amid 2028 presidential buzz, joking about his weight loss and taking sharp jabs at Trump.

news_hits.title: Illinois leads the way on resistance
news_hits.summary: Illinois' response to Trump's immigration crackdown — with Gov. Pritzker coordinating with Democratic governors and Mayor Brandon Johnson issuing executive orders limiting ICE's reach following Operation Midway Blitz — is being cited as a blueprint for Democratic resistance nationally.

news_hits.title: Property taxes, driven by TIF districts and school funds, outpace inflation and wages: county treasurer study
news_hits.summary: Cook County Treasurer Maria Pappas' report on skyrocketing property taxes — up 182% since 1995, twice the rate of inflation — is context for Alexi Giannoulias's uncontested Democratic path to November as Secretary of State. The report highlights state legislative failures that Giannoulias may need to address rhetorically as he campaigns statewide.

news_hits.title: King of Illinois: Pritzker swings Senate race as he targets Trump
news_hits.summary: Gov. Pritzker emerged as a kingmaker in the Illinois Democratic primary after backing Lt. Gov. Stratton to a Senate win. As an uncontested Democratic Secretary of State nominee, Giannoulias shares the same Pritzker-aligned Illinois Democratic slate heading into November.

news_hits.title: Alexi Giannoulias wins Illinois Secretary of State Democratic primary uncontested
news_hits.source: The New York Times / Associated Press
news_hits.summary: Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias advanced through the Democratic primary uncontested. On the Republican side, Diane M. Harris won the GOP Secretary of State primary with 53.0% (276K votes) over Walter Adamczyk (47.0%). Giannoulias faces Harris in November.

news_hits.title: No fooling, Illinois seeing a jobs surge
news_hits.summary: The April 2, 2026 Politico Illinois Playbook covers the Q1 2026 jobs surge and Illinois Democrats' post-primary positioning. Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias, who ran unopposed in the Democratic primary and is sitting on $9M in campaign funds, is widely mentioned in connection with a potential 2027 Chicago mayoral run — the April post-primary period is when his positioning will begin to come into focus.

news_hits.title: Illinois investors to receive nearly $950,000 in multi-state brokerage settlement
news_hits.summary: Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias secured nearly $950,000 for Illinois investors as part of a $19 million multi-state settlement involving five major brokerage firms accused of charging excessive commissions on small-dollar stock trades. The action underscores Giannoulias's investor protection mandate as he heads into a November reelection contest against Republican Diane Harris.

news_hits.title: Alexi Giannoulias wins Illinois Secretary of State Democratic primary uncontested; faces Republican Diane Harris
news_hits.summary: Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias ran uncontested in the Illinois Democratic primary and advances to the November general election against Republican Diane Harris. Giannoulias's $9M war chest and incumbency advantage make him a strong favorite in November. His office also remains active on investor protection enforcement.

primary_result.note: Uncontested Dem SoS primary. Faces Diane Harris (R) in November.
